main_sections.title: Braille
main_sections.prose_summary: This informational text recounts the life and invention of Louis Braille. Louis was a three-year-old boy in France whose eye was accidentally pierced by a sharp tool in his father's blacksmith workshop; the injury spread to both eyes and he became totally blind. At age ten, in 1819, he was admitted to the Royal Institute for Young Blind Persons in Paris, where he learnt letters through tactile means. He encountered Captain Charles Barbier de la Serre's 'Night Writing' — raised dots and dashes allowing soldiers to read in the dark — but it was too complex to be widely adopted. Louis, as a teenager, simplified this system and created the Braille alphabet: one raised-dot symbol for each of the twenty-six letters of the English alphabet. Braille is read by touch, requires more space than print (so Braille books are thicker), and is open to anyone, not just people with blindness. The text ends with the inspiring line: 'Thus, a blind person taught the world to see.'

reading.section_name: Let us Read + Let us Think A

tasks_verbatim.task_text: Let us Read: Read the informational text 'Braille' (pp. 55–57). Includes a diagram of the full Braille alphabet (a–z) showing raised-dot patterns. New Words: blacksmith, blind, pincushions, invented.

visual_stimulus.brief_description: Full Braille alphabet chart showing raised-dot patterns for all 26 letters (a–z) arranged in a book-spread layout

tasks_verbatim.task_text:
Answer the following.
1. Describe the Braille alphabet.
2. Why was 'Night Writing' not a success?
3. What did Louis Braille do to make reading easier for the blind?

question_bank.stem: Describe the Braille alphabet.
Why was 'Night Writing' not a success?
What did Louis Braille do to make reading easier for the blind?

oracy.section_name: Let us Think B + Let us Speak + Let us Listen

tasks_verbatim.task_text:
Think and discuss.
1. You may have seen people with blindness walking with a white cane. Why is the cane always white-coloured?
2. Imagine that the world becomes dark for a day and there is no light. What difficulties might one face on such a day?

question_bank.stem: You may have seen people with blindness walking with a white cane. Why is the cane always white-coloured?
Imagine that the world becomes dark for a day and there is no light. What difficulties might one face on such a day?

tasks_verbatim.task_text:
Close your eyes. Pick an object. Keep the object back. Now complete the sentences and share them with the class.
1. The object in my hand was ___ in shape.
2. The object in my hand was ___ (longer/shorter) than my forefinger.
3. The object in my hand was a ___.
Now add two more lines to describe the object.

question_bank.stem: The object in my hand was ___ in shape.
The object in my hand was ___ (longer/shorter) than my forefinger.
The object in my hand was a ___. Now add two more lines to describe the object.

tasks_verbatim.task_text: Let us Listen: Your teacher will read out the following words. Listen carefully and repeat: spoon, splinter, spin, splash, speak, split, space, splendid.

question_bank.stem: Listen and repeat: spoon, splinter, spin, splash, speak, split, space, splendid. (Focus on consonant cluster 'spl'.)

tasks_verbatim.transcript_text: Words to be read aloud by teacher (stress on 'spl' cluster, repeated 3–4 times each): spoon — splinter — spin — splash — speak — split — space — splendid. Teacher emphasises the consonant cluster 'spl' and gives learners time to familiarise themselves with the sound before repeating.

word_work.section_name: Let us Learn C + Let us Learn D

tasks_verbatim.task_text:
Read the following sentences and notice the underlined words: 'Long ago, in France, a little boy named Louis Braille was playing near his home.' / 'The cats were sleeping when I reached home.' / 'Sameer was playing hopscotch last evening.' All the above sentences are examples of events that started in the past and continued for some time. If the sentence is about one person, animal or bird, we use 'was + action word + ing.' If the sentence is about more than one, we use 'were + action word + ing.' The following sentences describe actions in the present. Change them to the past as shown in the examples.
Present → Past:
I am sleeping. → I was sleeping.
The dogs are barking at the stranger. → ___
Grandfather is searching for his spectacles. → ___
Sheeba is writing a letter. → ___
He is singing a song. → ___
We are playing kho-kho. → ___
The man is using his hands to push his wheelchair. → ___

question_bank.stem: Change to past: The dogs are barking at the stranger.
Change to past: Grandfather is searching for his spectacles.
Change to past: Sheeba is writing a letter.
Change to past: He is singing a song.
Change to past: We are playing kho-kho.
Change to past: The man is using his hands to push his wheelchair.

tasks_verbatim.task_text: Look at the picture story. Now fill in the blanks from the suitable options (was clapping, was scattering, were sitting, was hanging, was entering, was feeding): My friend Tarandeep told me an interesting thing yesterday. She ___ her friends, the squirrels and the sparrows. She ___ grains for them. Her grandmother ___ out the clothes to dry on a clothesline. Grandmother ___ the house when a strong wind began to blow. When she returned with the clothes pegs, she saw a wonderful scene! The sparrows and squirrels ___ on the clothes to prevent them from falling. Tarandeep ___ her hands with joy. Grandmother put the pegs on the clothes, and Tarandeep set down a bowl of water for her friends.

question_bank.stem: Fill in the blanks with the correct past continuous form from: was clapping, was scattering, were sitting, was hanging, was entering, was feeding.
question_bank.table:
Blank|Options
She ___ her friends (squirrels and sparrows)|was feeding
She ___ grains for them|was scattering
Her grandmother ___ out the clothes|was hanging
Grandmother ___ the house|was entering
The sparrows and squirrels ___ on the clothes|were sitting
Tarandeep ___ her hands with joy|was clapping

visual_stimulus.brief_description: Five-panel picture story showing a girl (Tarandeep) feeding squirrels and sparrows while grandmother hangs clothes; wind blows; animals sit on clothes to hold them down; Tarandeep claps with joy

writing.section_name: Let us Write A + Let us Write B

tasks_verbatim.task_text:
Write these words using the Braille alphabet. You can paste 'bindis'. One is done for you.
1. Cat  2. Man  3. Boy  4. Jug  5. Write your name in Braille.

question_bank.stem: Write the word 'Cat' using the Braille alphabet dot patterns (bindis).
Write the word 'Man' using the Braille alphabet dot patterns (bindis).
Write the word 'Boy' using the Braille alphabet dot patterns (bindis).
Write the word 'Jug' using the Braille alphabet dot patterns (bindis).
Write your name in Braille using the dot patterns (bindis).

tasks_verbatim.task_text: Louis Braille lost his eyesight in an accident. Some time ago, a newspaper reported that a child lost one eye while lighting firecrackers. List the precautions that should be taken to protect eyes from harm.

question_bank.stem: List the precautions that should be taken to protect eyes from harm.

beyond_text.section_name: Let us Do + Let us Explore

tasks_verbatim.task_text: 1. Take a piece of paper. With the help of a matchstick, or the back of a pencil, press hard on the paper to 'draw' a circle, a plus sign, or a letter of the alphabet. Now, close your eyes, turn the page over, and run your finger on the raised surface on the paper. Can you make out what the shape or outline is? Now, try the same activity with your friend. You may also use white clay for the same.

question_bank.stem: Press a circle/plus sign/letter on paper using a matchstick or pencil back; close eyes and feel the raised surface. Can you identify the shape? Try with a friend. White clay may also be used.

tasks_verbatim.task_text: 2. There are some children with low vision. (e.g., wearing thick glasses or holding a book too close to their eyes). Being a classmate and friend, how can you assist them? For example: Bringing a printout of a paragraph with large fonts to help them read.

question_bank.stem: Being a classmate and friend, how can you assist children with low vision? (For example: Bringing a printout of a paragraph with large fonts to help them read.)

tasks_verbatim.task_text: Let us Explore: Find out about the nearest resource centre for people with blindness.

question_bank.stem: Find out about the nearest resource centre for people with blindness.
